themes.name: Stuck in the Mud
themes.problem: Clients are 'withdrawn' from business development due to fatigue, fear of loss or lack of clarity & belief.
themes.solution: Create self relevance by identifying with a persona; let them 'see themselves' within the Heald matrix & see that progress is easily within their reach.

themes.name: The Knowledge Gap
themes.problem: Clients understand 'money in vs money out' in a linear fashion; they do not understand the true effects of time and discounting.
themes.solution: Create the ah-ha moment; let clients see their business in cyclic terms. Clients will then piece together the role of forecasting & budgeting in their business.

themes.name: The Wake Up Call
themes.problem: Clients accept 'ups & downs' but don't have any way of quantifying their range; leading to poor data conclusions.
themes.solution: Allow clients to see that a business is a system (collection of processes) & that a reduction in volatility can only come after refining the business procedures.

themes.name: Eyes On The Prize
themes.problem: Clients carry too many ideas in their 'neck-top computer'; they need a place to 'unpack their thoughts' so future moves become clear.
themes.solution: Encourage clients to see problems & opportunities as a series of 'what-if' events; logic supports ideas & quantifies what's needed to be successful.

themes.name: Test & Learn
themes.problem: If clients view a financial forecast as a 'static prediction' they fall victim to feelings of judgement & frustration. They miss the joy that comes from learning.
themes.solution: A 3 way forecast combined with 'revenue model sensitivity' gives very strong clues as to what to expect going forward. Finally, the owner can feel in control.

themes.name: Measure What Matters
themes.problem: Clients view financial reporting as being somewhat disconnected from how their business 'works'. Accordingly most of their business decisions are simply based on current bank balance and inventory levels.
themes.solution: The dashboard discussion enables the advisor to act as a 'financial translator'. The accounts are explained as 'Variable' + Activity Based + Fixed (to keep things simple) and relate to Winning Jobs, Doing Jobs and Being Resourced.

themes.name: Under the Microscope
themes.problem: If clients aren't sure what they can do (in practical terms) upon reviewing their performance, there's little point agreeing to regular meetings (or they won't last long even if they do agree).
themes.solution: Once we remove the notion of judgement from our financial management discussions, we're free to embrace systematic improvement based on data.
